candidates.comparison: computed sum vs expected constant (0+1+2=3), before and after sleep/wake
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: no — torch.allclose is a tolerance-based assertion on deterministic integer arithmetic, not generated model output
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: no — integer zero_() and += operations on tensors are exact; no FP accumulation drift
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: no — Strong Contract #2: kernel tolerance tests where the tolerance IS the contract; also Strong Contract #5: sleep/wake restoration
candidates.code_snippet:
output = x + y + z
assert torch.allclose(output, torch.ones_like(output) * 3)

candidates.comparison: CUDA graph replay output vs expected (x @ I + 1 = x + 1), and cache content vs input
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: no — torch.allclose on deterministic identity matrix multiplication, not generated model output
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: no — identity matrix multiplication is exact; FP drift cannot change x @ I
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: no — Strong Contract #2: kernel tolerance test; Strong Contract #4: eager vs cudagraph for same execution
candidates.code_snippet:
x.random_()
model_graph.replay()
assert torch.allclose(x, cache[: x.size(0)])
assert torch.allclose(y, x + 1)

candidates.comparison: output before sleep vs output after wake_up (full and partial wake_up)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — exact string == on generated text
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: no — sleep/wake restores the same weights to GPU; same request, same engine state, same kernels
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: no — Strong Contract #5: sleep/wake restoration should not change model math
candidates.code_snippet:
output = llm.generate(prompt, sampling_params)
llm.sleep(level=1)
llm.wake_up()
output2 = llm.generate(prompt, sampling_params)
assert output[0].outputs[0].text == output2[0].outputs[0].text

candidates.comparison: output before deep sleep vs output after wake_up + reload_weights
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — exact string == on generated text
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: no — deep sleep + reload restores same weights from checkpoint; same request, same engine state
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: no — Strong Contract #5: sleep/wake restoration and reload should not change model math
candidates.code_snippet:
output = llm.generate(prompt, sampling_params)
llm.sleep(level=2)
llm.wake_up(tags=['weights'])
llm.collective_rpc('reload_weights')
llm.wake_up(tags=['kv_cache'])
output2 = llm.generate(prompt, sampling_params)
assert output[0].outputs[0].text == output2[0].outputs[0].text

candidates.comparison: output before deep sleep vs output after wake_up + reload_weights (async engine)
candidates.c1_weak_oracle: yes — exact string == on generated text
candidates.c2_realistic_breakage: no — deep sleep + reload restores same weights; async vs sync engine path does not change the math
candidates.c3_no_strong_contract: no — Strong Contract #5: sleep/wake restoration and reload should not change model math
candidates.code_snippet:
outputs = llm.generate(prompt, sampling_params, request_id='test_request_id1')
async for output in outputs: pass
await llm.sleep(level=2)
await llm.wake_up(tags=['weights'])
await llm.collective_rpc('reload_weights')
await llm.wake_up(tags=['kv_cache'])
outputs2 = llm.generate(prompt, sampling_params, request_id='test_request_id2')
async for output2 in outputs2: pass
assert output.outputs[0].text == output2.outputs[0].text
